Rows.Name: Forest_Wolf
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Wolf-Lore1", "Genetically engineered Eurasian wolf, designed for various climates. Most common predator within terraformed environments.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Wolf-Lore2", "Wolves are skilled pack hunters that are capable of taking down prey many times their size. The subspecies introduced to Icarus are far more aggressive than those on Earth, and become easily fixated on humans who wander into their territory.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Wolf-Lore3", "\"Chills. Down. Your. Spine. You try getting to sleep while a ghastly choir of howling surrounds ya makeshift camp. Its downright unsettling.\"")

Rows.Name: Snow_Wolf
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Wolf-Lore1", "Genetically engineered Eurasian wolf, designed for a harsh and cold climate. The most common predator within terraformed environments.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Wolf-Lore2", "Wolves are skilled pack hunters that are capable of taking down prey many times their size. The subspecies introduced to Icarus are far more aggressive than those on Earth, and become easily fixated on humans who wander into their territory.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Wolf-Lore3", "\"Chills. Down. Your. Spine. You try getting to sleep while a ghastly choir of howling surrounds ya makeshift camp. Its downright unsettling.\"")

Rows.Name: Bear_Cub
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear_Cub-Lore1", "These cubs are usually found in the forest and wooded areas of Icarus, guarded by their often easily-provoked mothers.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear_Cub-Lore2", "An unnamed UDA researcher made a failed attempt to tame Bear cubs from birth, in the hope that they could be used as mounts for prospectors travelling large distances. Needless to say, they did not succeed, or survive.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear_Cub-Lore3", "\"I mean, they\'re cute and all but... a mans gotta eat. I\'m not proud of it, but sometimes these little fellas are all that stand between me and starvation.\"")

Rows.Name: Bear
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear-Lore1", "Unlike Bears on earth, Icarus Ursus horribilis have no need to hibernate. They also produce offspring at a much higher rate. ")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear-Lore2", "Introduced to maintain reasonable levels of herbivore fauna.  While originally engineered to be more passive unless food motivated, the Bear consistently displays levels of increased aggression towards prospectors. The UDA is not liable for missing appendages, limbs or crewmates. ")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bear-Lore3", "\"Not your first thought. One of the biggest initial dangers being bears.... You start to take it seriously after a crewmate is taken or mauled. You know they ******* start eating from the lower half of your damn body? I\'ll.... I\'ll never get the screams out of my head\"")

Rows.Name: Chamios
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios-Lore1", "Chamois have a mix of deer and antelope qualities, and will flee at the first sign of a predator.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios-Lore2", "Championed by a French sect of the UDA, the Chamois was introduced to Icarus to encourage diversity, and provide easy game for ill-prepared prospectors.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios-Lore3", "\"AH the ol\' shammy. Stupid little goat thing. Bear fodder. Feeder of wolves. Meat on legs.\"")

Rows.Name: Elephant
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Elephant-Lore1", "This species was hunted to near-extinction on earth. and was introduced to Icarus as a means of regenerating their population.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Elephant-Lore2", "Found In Arid environments, the African Elephant was genetically modified to make them less desirable to poachers. Alterations were made to the structure of their tusks causing them to degrade over time, when separated from the elephant. Effectively halting the ivory trade.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Elephant-Lore3", "\"Something was... lost in the genetic reconstruction of this species. African Elephants on earth are intelligent, self-aware creatures. In multiple instances they\'ve passed the MSR test. Icarus Loxodonta do not react as expected when presented with the mirror test. We did... I... something isn\'t right.\"")

Rows.Name: Polar_Bear_Cub
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear_Cub-Lore1", "Found in colder parts of Icarus, these tiny versions of their parents are generally considered to be easy pickings for hungry predators... and prospectors.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear_Cub-Lore2", "Polar bear cubs are born blind and deaf, making them heavily dependent on their mothers for the first few months of life.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear_Cub-Lore3", "Do I wish I could keep one as a pet? Absolutely. Would it be likely to tear my face off in my sleep? Absolutely.")

Rows.Name: Polar_Bear
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear-Lore1", "Polar bears can be found in colder areas of the Terrazone. Originally seeded to promote biodiversity in the Icarus food chain. Due to their increased aggression and predatory instincts they quickly wiped out the seal population.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear-Lore2", "Valued by prospectors for their meat but especially for their fur which can be used to craft armor to protect against the harsh climates of Icarus. Generally avoided by novice prospectors.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Polar_Bear-Lore3", "\"Valued by prospectors for their meat but especially for their fur which can be used to craft armor to protect against the harsh climates of Icarus. Generally avoided by novice prospectors.\"")

Rows.Name: Baby_Deer
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Baby_Deer-Lore1", "Infant deer, commonly seen following their parent through the Icarus wilderness. Has been known to follow other creatures if it\'s parent dies.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Baby_Deer-Lore2", "Widely considered by prospectors to be one of the most convenient sources of food on Icarus. Killing Baby Deer contributes to the Heartless Accolade.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Baby_Deer-Lore3", "\"Bro. Every ol\' mate and his dog have had to kill these little critters. It\'s survival. If you\'re hurt... tired or slow... They\'re easier to hunt... But the sound they make when they die... A heavy meal in more ways than one.\"")

Rows.Name: Forest_Deer
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Deer-Lore1", "On Icarus, deer make up the most abundant population of herbivores. Typically, they can be found in family groups of three or more. As they are sizable and fleet-footed creatures, they are prone to getting spooked easily and are adept at camouflaging themselves in the forest.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Deer-Lore2", "A black market for Icarus venison has been long suspected. While prospectors aren\'t supposed to remove any biological matter from the surface, rumors of Icarus meat and produce appearing on earth have been circulating. UDA INTSEC are currently investigating.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Forest_Deer-Lore3", "\"It just tastes better. Earth feed is crap in comparison... Sure we have chem residue and exotics, but not decades of pollution and microplastics... Hah... I might grow a third leg, and I\'m okay with that, so long as I don\'t have to eat that printed earth-side ****.\"")

Rows.Name: Desert_Deer
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Deer-Lore1", "On Icarus, Antelope make up the most abundant population of herbivores along with their deer cousins. Typically, they can be found in family groups of three or more. As they are sizable and fleet-footed creatures, they are prone to getting spooked easily and are adept at camouflaging themselves in the desert.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Deer-Lore2", "A black market for Icarus venison has been long suspected. While prospectors aren\'t supposed to remove any biological matter from the surface, rumors of Icarus meat and produce appearing on earth have been circulating. UDA INTSEC currently investigating.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Deer-Lore3", "\"It just tastes better. Earth feed is crap in comparison... Sure we have chem residue and exotics, but not decades of pollution and microplastics... I might grow a third leg, and I\'m okay with that, so long as I don\'t have to eat that printed earth-side ****.\"")

Rows.Name: Cougar
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cougar-Lore1", "The cougar subspecies was brought in to control the population of smaller mammals and adapted quickly to the arid regions of Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cougar-Lore2", "Much like deer antlers on Earth, experienced prospectors like to show off mounted Cougar heads as a sign of their hunting and survival abilities.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cougar-Lore3", "\"I had to treat a Prospectors severe lacerations on MULTIPLE occasions. They continually attempted to feed and pet large feline species.... Claimed to have discovered a new \'kitty cat\' species in the fringe zone.... I\'ve not heard from them in a while.\"")

Rows.Name: Mammoth
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mammoth-Lore1", "Revived ancient earth species. A well preserved sample was found deep in the Le Brea tar pits, the first few mammoths were carried by elephants as surrogate mothers. Due to significant debate on the ethics of the mammoths revival, the experiment had to be conducted on Icarus. ")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mammoth-Lore2", "Extremist group TPS was responsible for the organisation of global protests against the revival of this species. While this study was not the most advanced genetics experiment, it became a pivotal reference in arguments against genetic augmentation. A slippery slope to experimentation on humans.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mammoth-Lore3", "\"While we appreciate new technology that allows us to maintain a healthy ecosystem, the potential of this technology being used unethically... more than likely it already is used unethically. We deserve better, you deserve better.\" - TPS Spokesperson")

Rows.Name: Jaguar
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Jaguar-Lore1", "The jaguar was introduced to regulate the population of smaller mammals and quickly adapted to forest and wooded areas in the terraformed zones of Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Jaguar-Lore2", "Jaguars are solitary and territorial animals that are primarily active at dawn and dusk. They are skilled hunters and prey on a variety of animals, including chamois, deer, and nosy prospectors.. no matter how much of a \'cat person\' they may be.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Jaguar-Lore3", "\"Tried to make a BIG ball of fiber hoping this little cutie would play with it. Didn\'t work out as well as I had hoped... But hey. You only need one kidney.\"")

Rows.Name: Pronghorn
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pronghorn-Lore1", "Pronghorns were originally introduced to Icarus to add variety to the population and give prospectors a good challenge at target practice.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pronghorn-Lore2", "Pronghorns are incredibly fast runners, which makes them difficult prey for predators. They also have excellent eyesight and can detect movement miles away. However, they are not as agile as other animals, which can make them vulnerable to predators in certain situations.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pronghorn-Lore3", "\"I swear. It\'s like they have magnets in their horns. You spend ages stalk\'n them, get up as close as you can...  line up your shot... THUNK! Arrow falls to the ground.  Looks like I\'m going hungry tonight.\"")

Rows.Name: Snow_Leopard
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Leopard-Lore1", "Snow leopards are large, muscular cats with thick, white fur that helps them blend into their snowy mountain habitat. They have distinctive black spots and rosettes on their fur, and their long, bushy tail provides balance and warmth in cold temperatures.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Leopard-Lore2", "Snow leopards are solitary animals. They are highly adapted to their mountain habitat and are able to climb steep, rocky terrain with ease. They are also skilled hunters and prey on a variety of animals, but are often killed for their valuable fur.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Leopard-Lore3", "\"Aren\'t they just the fuzziest? I\'ve had these two following me for the last few days, some would say they\'re just exhibiting standard hunting behaviour but me? I know better. I swear they are this close to accepting me into their furry little family, I can just feel it... I wonder if an orbital laser would work for playtime...\"")

Rows.Name: Rabbit
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit-Lore1", "They have large, furry feet that provide traction on icy surfaces, and long, floppy ears that help regulate their body temperature. They are very vulnerable to predators, and easily startled.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit-Lore2", "These are social animals that live in groups called herds,  spending much of their time foraging for food in the snow. They are herbivores and feed on a variety of plants.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit-Lore3", "\"Quick little critters keep gettin\' into my food stores. If they keep mucking me around I\'ll see if they have better manners as a pair of socks.\"")

Rows.Name: Desert_Wolf
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Wolf-Lore1", "These medium-sized carnivores have distinctive spotted fur and powerful jaws. They are often thought of as scavengers but they are also skilled hunters.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Wolf-Lore2", "Considered the pranksters of Icarus, these creatures are known to attack prospectors for no discernible reason and then laugh at their victims.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Wolf-Lore3", "\"You think the wolves are bad? I had two of them Hyena things circling my base the other day. Howling is easy to ignore but the laughing? That just drives me MAD.\"")

Rows.Name: Buffalo
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Buffalo-Lore1", "Buffalo are large, muscular mammals with shaggy brown fur and a distinctive hump on their shoulders. They have short, curved horns and a broad, flat face with a large, black nose.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Buffalo-Lore2", "Buffalo are social animals that can be tamed young by prospectors with a bit of patience. They spend much of their day grazing on grasses and other vegetation. They are also excellent swimmers and can cross rivers and other bodies of water.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Buffalo-Lore3", "\"Great big ol\' lumbering things. At first I thought they were only good for eatin\', then I realized you could tame em! And heck if didn\'t that make things ten times easier on this dang rock.\"")

Rows.Name: Wild_Boar
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wild_Boar-Lore1", "Wild boar are medium-sized mammals with a stocky build, short legs, and bristly hair. They are typically brown or black in color and have curved tusks that protrude from their mouths.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wild_Boar-Lore2", "Wild boar are strong and aggressive animals that can defend themselves against predators such as wolves and bears. They are also excellent runners and swimmers. However, they can be a nuisance to prospectors.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wild_Boar-Lore3", "\"Space bacon. My favourite part of this cursed rock.\"")

Rows.Name: Zebra
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Zebra-Lore1", "Zebras are social animals that live in herds. They are herbivores and use their stripes as a form of camouflage to confuse predators.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Zebra-Lore2", "This particular breed of Zebra are the direct result of the UDA engineering labs. Sample tissue has shown evidence of significant genetic divergence. It\'s theorized that continued exposure to exotic matter and/or terraforming enzymes may result in severe mutation.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Zebra-Lore3", "\"The real question is: are they white with black stripes? Or are they black with white stripes? Steve bet against me and he still owes me from last time...\"")

Rows.Name: Kea
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kea-Lore1", "A large, intelligent parrot, originally native to New Zealand. On Earth, the Kea population continues to steadily decline. Seeding on Icarus was seen as a way to keep the species alive. Genetically modified to handle harsher conditions.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kea-Lore2", "Dr Amelia Tohu personally oversaw this project, having spent time raising Kea in conservation sectors as a young girl. Tohu was uncompromising in her commitment to propagating the species.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kea-Lore3", "\"Careful of the cheeky buggers. They\'re not dangerous or anything, but they\'ll steal food right out from under ya nose. Hell, they\'ll rip the seals right off your dropship windows.\"")

Rows.Name: Crocodile
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Crocodile-Lore1", "Crocodiles are large, carnivorous reptiles with a long, powerful tail and a broad, armored body. They have sharp teeth and a distinctive, elongated snout.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Crocodile-Lore2", "Crocodiles are solitary animals that spend much of their time basking in the sun or submerged in water. They are ambush predators that lie in wait for prey, which includes fish, birds, and mammals.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Crocodile-Lore3", "\"Crocodiles are... easy. They try to kill and eat you. Now the UDA... That\'s a reptile to be afraid of.\"")

Rows.Name: Komodo
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Komodo-Lore1", "Komodo dragons are large, carnivorous reptiles with a long, powerful tail and a rough, scaly skin. They have sharp, curved teeth and a forked tongue.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Komodo-Lore2", "Komodo dragons are solitary animals that are active during the day. They are ambush predators that hunt prey such as deer, boar, and buffalo. They are also known to scavenge carrion.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Komodo-Lore3", "\"Damn things have a cesspool in their mouths. Some... Fifty different bacterial strains. Handful of which are exceedingly septic. AND a venom gland in their lower jaw. WHY.\"")

Rows.Name: Desert_Scorpion
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Scorpion-Lore1", "Experiment run by Dr Lee. The assumption \'Scorpions are just land lobster\' was subject of much discussion. Dr Lee wanted to prove this fast reproducing, meaty arachnid, could be a source of food for prospectors in arid environments.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Scorpion-Lore2", "Due to Prospectors having an extreme aversion to dealing with these enlarged scorpions, this line of research had it\'s funding cut. Somewhat suspiciously, despite the project being canceled, a biological containment unit was discovered planet-side. Seeding the large scorpions on Icarus. Dr Lee is currently under investigation.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Scorpion-Lore3", "\"This doesn\'t taste like lobster. **** are they thinking.\"")

Rows.Name: Arctic_Scorpion
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Scorpion-Lore1", "Experiment run by Dr Lee. The assumption \'Scorpions are just land lobster\' was subject of much discussion. Dr Lee wanted to prove this fast reproducing, meaty arachnid, could be a source of food for prospectors in colder environments.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Scorpion-Lore2", "Due to Prospectors having an extreme aversion to dealing with these enlarged scorpions, this line of research had it\'s funding cut. Somewhat suspiciously, despite the project being canceled, a biological containment unit was discovered planet-side. Seeding the large scorpions on Icarus. Dr Lee is currently under investigation.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Scorpion-Lore3", "\"This doesn\'t taste like lobster. **** are they thinking?!\"")

Rows.Name: Caveworm
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Caveworm-Lore1", "Commonly found in cave systems all across Icarus. This Infant Sandworm is believed to be the second stage of the Worm life cycle.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Caveworm-Lore2", "Freshly hatched, these larva like worms use Icarus cave systems as a relatively safe environment to grow. Scientists believe the mineral rich habitat contributes to this growth. ")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Caveworm-Lore3", "\"Cave clearing. We all have to do it some time... Though... They\'re like roaches, always come back eventually.\"")

Rows.Name: Teenage_Caveworm
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Teenage_Caveworm-Lore1", "Adolescent Sandworms that have outgrown the nest. Found all over Icarus, they spend most of their time deep underground.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Teenage_Caveworm-Lore2", "It\'s theorized - due to the relative prevalence of adolescents and the significantly smaller numbers of fully grown worms, that many do not live past this stage.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Teenage_Caveworm-Lore3", "\"We think they only surface to hunt... It\'s terrifying to think how many could be under our feet as we speak. Since they spend so much time underground, we just don\'t know much about them\"")

Rows.Name: Moa
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Moa-Lore1", "Found in moderate terrazone environments. This previously extinct creature had many variants. Specifically found on Icarus is the Dinornis novaezealandiae, the second largest moa species in recorded history.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Moa-Lore2", "This large flightless bird is the result of genetic experimentation, preserved Moa DNA was found in amber near Tekapo, New Zealand. This was used to revive the extinct species.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Moa-Lore3", "\"Life will find a way... Directly into a prospectors stomach.\"")

Rows.Name: Alpha_Wolf_Boss
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Alpha_Wolf_Boss-Lore1", "Genetically engineered Eurasian wolf, the strongest of its species. Larger and more aggressive than the average wolf found on Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Alpha_Wolf_Boss-Lore2", "Highly territorial, this species will not give up a hunt easily. Exhibits denning behavior, will often be found surrounded by it\'s pack.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Alpha_Wolf_Boss-Lore3", "\"I don\'t know which one of those crackpot UDA gene jocks hates us prospectors... but whoever made this thing is sadistic.\"")

Rows.Name: Landshark_Boss
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Landshark_Boss-Lore1", "A larger sandworm that\'s been sighted occasionally in various environments. Displays a handful of unique physical and behavioral traits beyond what prospectors have experienced during other sandworm encounters.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Landshark_Boss-Lore2", "There have been multiple reports of this creature appearing during induced seismic events associated with terraforming. This creature may be another stage in the worm life cycle, it\'s rarity has made it difficulty to study.  The UDA is currently forming various kill and capture strike teams.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Landshark_Boss-Lore3", "\"This is exactly the kind of aberration I\'ve talked about before. It displays such unique biology... Everything is comparatively simple on earth... I\'m sure its genome contains a breakthrough genetic key... We just need more sample material\" - Dr Amelia Tohu")

Rows.Name: Scorpion_Boss
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Scorpion_Boss-Lore1", "The result of engineered scorpions being accidentally released on Icarus. It\'s likely exposure to environmental conditions in addition to exotic material that caused extreme genetic mutation.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Scorpion_Boss-Lore2", "Unlike most scorpions, this giant arachnid is territorial. It prefers a large plateau to nest, it\'s theorised that it may flatten it\'s surroundings to create an ideal hunting ground.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Scorpion_Boss-Lore3", "\"She is perfect. I\'ve been a practicing geneticist over a long 25 year career. I can only hope, one day, to create something so beautiful.\" - Dr Lee")

Rows.Name: Sandworm
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandworm-Lore1", "Giant worm-like creature native to Icarus. Partly to blame for the ubiquity of cave systems on the planet. Most common in arid areas - though it\'s theorized this creatures life cycle takes place over a variety of environments.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandworm-Lore2", "The Sandworm has been a focus of a plethora of studies. The UDA see these worms as a source of non-terrestrial biological technology. Chief Geneticist Dr Amelia Tohu has been tasked with gathering more information on the species.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandworm-Lore3", "\"We\'ve seen radical mutation in species we\'ve introduced into the terrazone. And over a relatively short period of time the change has been remarkable. Well, the Worms. This species is old. It\'s been exposed to exotic material for hundreds, maybe thousands of years. It\'s evolution is inseparable to this exposure. We must focus on learning more\" - Dr Amelia Tohu")

Rows.Name: Rockdog
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rockdog-Lore1", "A large predator with a rock-like outer coating that protects it from most damage.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rockdog-Lore2", "Stonejaw were one of the first subspecies to be discovered on Icarus as a result of exotic mutation. Scientists have yet to confirm if its bite has been enhanced with venom due to contractors being eaten before they have the chance to inspect their wounds.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rockdog-Lore3", "\"Buggers get more and more pissed off until they explode at someone. Frustrating as HELL to deal with.\"")

Rows.Name: BatDog
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "BatDog-Lore1", "A genetically mutated form of the Eurasian wolf, cause of the genetic aberration is unknown. Its vampire bat like features lead to prospectors informally referring to the creature as Dracula, eventually shortened to Drac.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "BatDog-Lore2", "The Drac has lost many of its wolf like traits. Its enlarged teeth and jaw musculature are a serious threat. It will occasionally howl to call for help when hunting. Due to an infrared reflecting film on the surface of their eyes, at a distance they can be seen glowing red.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "BatDog-Lore3", "\"I was assigned to Sci-ops in the fringe zone. Chasing some theory Dr Lee had about something living around pockets of molten lava. We always had our eyes on the horizon, searching for that tell tale red glow...\"")

Rows.Name: Needler
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Needler-Lore1", "A genetically mutated form of the Crocodile or Komodo. Its exact origins are unknown, but samples show earth species genetic markers.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Needler-Lore2", "Able to move swiftly underwater and deep in mud; this predator keeps its prey at range while showering its target with organic bone-like spines.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Needler-Lore3", "\"Good god, who thought it was a good idea to mix those genes together? I mean- HELLO? This thing is deadl-\"")

Rows.Name: Predator_Bird
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Predator_Bird-Lore1", "These birds can be found in the most inhospitable areas of Icarus. They feed on other creatures and are highly aggresive, making them disliked by contractors working in their territory.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Predator_Bird-Lore2", "Often mistaken for the relatively harmless Kea, the Dreadwing\'s hunting behaviour more closely resembles that of the Australian Magpie, it utilizes swift diving to attack its unfortunate prey.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Predator_Bird-Lore3", "\"First it\'s bears and then wolves and now we got these damn things dive-bombing us from the skies? I can\'t work in these conditions.\"")

Rows.Name: Gas_Flyer
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Gas_Flyer-Lore1", "Drifters were affectionately named after the way they float through the air, much like a jellyfish moves through water on Earth. They are generally passive creatures but will retaliate if threatened.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Gas_Flyer-Lore2", "Getting too close to this creature may cause them to attack, releasing a cloud of gas that acts similarly to a nerve agent. Some daring prospectors have taken to using this attack against other land-based creatures.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Gas_Flyer-Lore3", "\"They are strangely beautiful, nice to look at from a distance though. I wouldn\'t want to get too close to one, I quite like my brain the way it is.\"")

Rows.Name: Deer_Variant
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Deer_Variant-Lore1", "The Equix can be found in areas of Icarus with many different climates, it adapts to its surroundings swiftly and efficiently.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Deer_Variant-Lore2", "It is believed that the Equix is a mix of deer and earth antelope DNA, but the exact details are closely guarded by the UDA.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Deer_Variant-Lore3", "\"Blast me! I always thought my team were saying \'he\'s quick\', didn\'t know these critters had a real name\"")

Rows.Name: Swamp_Quadruped
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Quadruped-Lore1", "With their long legs and height these creatures move surprisingly swiftly when in marshy areas. They are generally passive unless provoked.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Quadruped-Lore2", "Believed to be endemic to Icarus, the Stryder seems purpose built for muddy terrain. They are timid creatures that will flee at the slightest provocation.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Quadruped-Lore3", "\"Cute lil turtle guys with their floppy eyes, running all over the damn place. No idea what to do with them, but they make my day each time I see \'em\"")

Rows.Name: Swamp_Bird
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Bird-Lore1", "The genetics team didn\'t quite get the size of the Ubis right, but as these creatures can be tamed (with some difficulty), many prospectors are more than happy about the mistake.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Bird-Lore2", "A genetic descendant of the Earth Flamingo, this creature has thrived since being introduced to Icarus. Instead of shrimp their colour is derived from the influence of nearby exotics.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Bird-Lore3", "\"Never thought much of Flamingos on earth... this fella though, I\'ve seen them knock a prospector out cold in one kick. Want some advice? Stay outta range.\"")

Rows.Name: Chamios_Variant
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios_Variant-Lore1", "Derived from the Capra family of goats on Earth, these herbivores are mostly pleasant-mannered and will flee from any noise or commotion.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios_Variant-Lore2", "Sadly when introduced to Icarus scientists noticed too late that this species has a habit of getting stuck in awkward places. Unlike their mountain goat bretheren back on Earth, the Chala tend to climb or explore and then accept their fate.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chamios_Variant-Lore3", "\"I don\'t care how \'safe\' they are, the eyes freak me out.\"")

Rows.Name: Blueback
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Blueback-Lore1", "The Blueback is an Icarus native, while mostly docile it will defend itself when provoked. Displays a symbiotic relationship with local and introduced fauna. When injured it will retreat into its shell, calling on the help of nearby predatory wildlife.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Blueback-Lore2", "Early results of study on the Blueback\'s call are captivating the scientific community. The call itself is a carrier wave of subsonic and hypersonic information. It seems to amplify aggression in any species with predatory instincts, and create a primal drive to follow the call. Group 15 are particularly interested in replicating this effect.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Blueback-Lore3", "\"We were told it wouldn\'t affect us. Bull****. I could feel it. Even at a huge distance... Almost a... pressure? Like a looming feeling that some bad **** will happen unless you follow.\"")

Rows.Name: Lava_Blueback
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Blueback-Lore1", "The Redback is an Icarus native, evolved for volcanic environments. While mostly docile it will defend itself when provoked. Displays a symbiotic relationship with local and introduced fauna. When injured it will retreat into its shell, calling on the help of nearby predatory wildlife. Believed to be a distant cousin of the Blueback.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Blueback-Lore2", "These creatures are well known for their distinctive calls, which are believed to be able to manipulate other creatures. This does include humans, as many prospectors have taken to defending Redbacks and keeping them as pets.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Blueback-Lore3", "\"Sure they look a bit weird at first but you get used to it, my girl Spot is just the cutest. The way she calls out for me and shakes her head with happiness, it just... makes me smile.\"")

Rows.Name: Snow_Striker
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Striker-Lore1", "The Stalker has a strong pack mentality, oriented around small family groups. The only creature native to Icarus to show such intense bonds. It\'s strong, agile and smart. The perfect hunter.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Striker-Lore2", "Stalkers are regarded as one of the more, if not the most frightening creatures found on Icarus. While there are larger, more aggressive creatures, the stalker is intelligent. Living up to their name, Prospectors report being followed for hours - days even, the stalker biding  its time to strike. Ambush behavior is common, it\'s highly recommended to request help when dealing with these creatures.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Striker-Lore3", "\"At first I thought it was all in my head, no one else thought we were being followed. But, sticking with my gut is why I\'m still here, and the rest of my team aren\'t. It starts as a flare of dread in your chest, if you feel a prickle down the back of your neck, then you\'re already too late.\"")

Rows.Name: Desert_Striker
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Striker-Lore1", "Arid Strikers are known for their extremely sharp claws, with an edge keen enough to gut a human in one blow, suit and all. ")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Striker-Lore2", "The Arid Striker is one of the reasons the UDA implemented the controversial \'buddy\' system. Prospectors with enough spare cash could choose a teammate to drop down with, in order to spot each other through Striker territory. The costs for this were astronomical, and the program was quickly scrapped.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Desert_Striker-Lore3", "\"I mean, have you SEEN the claws on those things?! I am never going into the Volcanic areas again. EVER.\"")

Rows.Name: Spider
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Spider-Lore1", "These giant arachnids were a botched pet project of Dr Lee. Geneticist and Arachnologist. Lee was repeatedly warned that many prospectors displayed an unwillingness to interact with the new species. The Doctor would adamantly reply \"But look at the little floofy nope feets\". Lee was not very popular.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Spider-Lore2", "Experimental arachnid gone wrong. A slightly larger arachnid was engineered to curb the spread of small endemic wildlife. Once introduced, environmental conditions and potentially exotic exposure caused this species to mutate. Avoid If possible.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Spider-Lore3", "\"**** THIS IS NOT OKAY, ****** THINGS SHOULD NOT EXIST! WHY THE **** DID SOMEONE MAKE THIS. I ******* QUIT, GET ME OFF THIS ******* PLANET. Why is it always spiders? Why couldn\'t it be butterflies?\"")

Rows.Name: Wooly_Zebra
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wooly_Zebra-Lore1", "These odd-looking creatures are believed to be a mutation directly resulting from the work done in Group 15 labs to integrate the genetically engineered Zebra into the Icarus food chain.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wooly_Zebra-Lore2", "It is theorised that this herd of Zebra quietly migrated into an unsupervised part of Icarus and were heavily affected by the surrounding exotic matter.  The team who stumbled across them on a recon mission of the area took several weeks to recover from the shock.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Wooly_Zebra-Lore3", "\"We thought we were prepared, they said there was nothing living in there. All of a sudden Tony turns around and runs past me and I come face to face with that... THING.  It\'s eyes... there was just something about it\'s eyes...\"")

Rows.Name: Roat
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Roat-Lore1", "One of the first creatures to be discovered outside the Earth-like Terrazone, the Roat is a small creature that is heavily adapted to its environment. Their temperament can change rapidly, catching many contractors off guard.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Roat-Lore2", "Prospectors have been warned by the UDA not to feed the Roats, although it is suspected that many ignore this warning. Much like seagulls on Earth, Roats can get quite aggressive when food is involved.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Roat-Lore3", "\"You get used to them after a while, funny little things. Some of them have quite the temper but I have found a few of them to be quite endearing... although that could be because I gave them half my dinner. Again.\"")

Rows.Name: Lava_Hunter_Spawns
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter_Spawns-Lore1", "These smaller specimens are the direct offspring of Lava Hunters, with tough skin that is incredibly resistant to heat.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter_Spawns-Lore2", "These creatures tend to work as a swarm to attack threats; flying, biting and generally wearing down their enemies over time.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter_Spawns-Lore3", "\"Agh- no- oof- get OFF me you gross little creepy thing- AHH it BIT me!\"")

Rows.Name: Swamp_Slug
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Slug-Lore1", "These gentle creatures are often found in swampy parts of Icarus, and are easily frightened by loud noises and disturbances in their vicinity. In most instances they will flee from humans.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Slug-Lore2", "The Viscid is generally harmless but can be highly territorial. They tend to leave slime trails around their territory; favouring large bodies of water. They have also been known to leave trails around man-made structures, this has resulted in a handful of contractors being evacuated to orbit with life-threatening injuries after accidentally stepping onto a trail.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Swamp_Slug-Lore3", "\"Like a trail of snot, couldn\'t believe it when I woke up and looked out my window and this lil fella was circling my base. Needless to say I got out of there pretty quickly, really miss that base though...\"")

Rows.Name: Lava_Hunter
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter-Lore1", "This mysterious creature is mainly known for its size and the speed of its attack. Rumours abound in regards to its origins though many prospectors consider them to be the guardians of Prometheus and stay well away from their nests.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter-Lore2", "These creatures were first discovered by a Group 15 scientist posted at the only volcanic station on Icarus. After a tiring day of exploration and surveying it is believed they strayed off course on the return to their base. They managed to send a blurry and frantic stream to Group 15 HQ before the feed was abruptly cut off. Their body was never found.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Hunter-Lore3", "\"I mean, I know us prospectors aren\'t the most creative bunch but this name just makes sense. It lives in lava and it hunts literally anything that moves. And the sound it makes? I\'ve still been getting nightmares from it months later... \"")

Rows.Name: Lava_Komodo
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Komodo-Lore1", "These creatures have developed a remarkable resistance to the harsh heat and toxic environments of the most hostile parts of Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Komodo-Lore2", "The thick hide of the Ashen Drake works to protect it from the searing heat of the ground it walks on, along with providing excellent defense from other predators.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Komodo-Lore3", "\"There I was just minding my own business trying to make it past the lava floes and then WHACK! This thing comes outta nowhere and almost knocked me in. Cheeky git.\"")

Rows.Name: Lava_Slug
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Slug-Lore1", "These gentle creatures are often found in the hotter parts of Icarus, and are easily frightened by loud noises and disturbances in their vicinity. In most instances they will flee from humans.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Slug-Lore2", "The Ignari is generally harmless but can be highly territorial. They tend to leave slime trails around their territory. They have also been known to leave trails around man-made structures, this has resulted in a handful of contractors being evacuated to orbit with life-threatening injuries after accidentally stepping onto a trail.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Lava_Slug-Lore3", "\"Like a trail of snot, couldn\'t believe it when I woke up and looked out my window and this lil fella was circling my base. Needless to say I got out of there pretty quickly, really miss that base though...\"")

Rows.Name: Rabbit_Variant
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit_Variant-Lore1", "Believed to be a mutation of the earth-like Rabbit, this creature is known for its uncanny ability to sense danger from a great distance.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit_Variant-Lore2", "The Pygmy Lop is another creature that the UDA has released a warning for, as studies have shown that they cannot be tamed or kept as a pet.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Rabbit_Variant-Lore3", "\"Don\'t pet the fluffy little darlings?? Why on earth would I listen to that rubbish? This one here is called Imelda and her friend over here is called Twiggy. They help me to finish off my dinner each night and in return I , uh, ATTEMPT to give them some snuggles.\"")

Rows.Name: Horse
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse-Lore1", "The Terrenus is an engineered descendant of Earth\'s horses.  Its compact, sturdy build allows for swift movement through most areas. Sinotai came to find that genetic splicing with Eurasian wild boar resulted in significantly heightened endurance in these creatures. Unlike their predecessors, the Terrenus is less likely to flee a predator - using its tusks to defend themselves and their kin.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse-Lore2", "Initial reports on Terrenus development showed concern regarding their ability to be trained for use on time-sensitive drops, where Contractors needed a reliable source of swift transportation. The first batch released into the terrazone disappeared within 72 hours, costing Sinotai a small fortune in lost revenue. The following group were fixed with tracking beacons, and a somewhat controversial device to keep them within a set radius of their designated work zone.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse-Lore3", "\"Moas. They\'re noisy and they **** everywhere. I\'m sick of em... These guys? WAY better. They may not be as fast, but it sure beats cleaning bird droppings all over your base.\"")

Rows.Name: Tusker
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tusker-Lore1", "The Tusker\'s four eyes help them to detect threats at a far greater accuracy than similar earth-like creatures such as the Bison. Capable of fighting off aggressive wildlife, the Tusker\'s increased stamina and deadly protrusions make it a strong choice for prospectors looking for a companion in the harsh wilderness of Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tusker-Lore2", "Found only in the coldest parts of the Terrazone, these creatures were discovered by an ACS scientist while setting up a forward base for weather monitoring. An avid storm chaser, their bravery led them to follow a family of Tuskers deep into the ice, documenting as much as they could before frostbite set in. A portion of the weather station was later converted into a ranch, where these animals are studied and tamed for use on longer prospects.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tusker-Lore3", "\"Once you get used to the two-sets-of-eyes thing these fellas are actually pretty cute. My latest one even fought off a wolf, I\'d like to see a Moa do that!\"")

Rows.Name: Arctic_Bat
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Bat-Lore1", "The Arctic Bat is a flying mammal adapted to life in freezing environments. These agile creatures navigate through vast cave systems with impressive echolocation abilities, preying on anything they can find in the cavernous darkness. ")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Bat-Lore2", "While nesting these bats can exhibit rather aggressive behaviour, to the extent that the Lagos Group has advised that contractors should leave any nests they find undisturbed. If left untreated, wounds from these creatures can cause severe distress, even death.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Bat-Lore3", "\"Oi, Rob, get over here! Reckon I\'ve found once of them nests they\'ve been harping on about. Hey, don\'t go near it, haven\'t you read the repor-\" Recording Terminated.")

Rows.Name: Bee
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bee-Lore1", "The Bees of Icarus are known for their long, needle-like stingers which are capable of piercing even the toughest of envirosuits. Proceed with caution. Despite the danger, they are still a reasonable source of food for many contractors living long-term on-planet.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bee-Lore2", "Bees were introduced to Icarus by the UDA in an attempt to boost local flora levels after the terraforming failed. Initially, they were deemed harmless and used by many prospectors as an easy source of food once a hive had been set up. However, the influence of exotics soon became apparent in their mutation from Earth insects into a far more dangerous Icarus variant.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bee-Lore3", "\"Just when you think your day can\'t get any worse... Bees. And not just any bees, there\'s no suit in orbit that can keep these little ****ers out. I swear they can smell fear.\"")

Rows.Name: Hammerhead_Slug
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hammerhead_Slug-Lore1", "The Hammerhead is believed to be an Icarus native, mainly found in the Prometheus region. They can be deceptively hard to kill, due to their tendency to split into smaller lifeforms to overwhelm their prey.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hammerhead_Slug-Lore2", "Hammerheads employ an arsenal of tricks to capture their prey, the most prominent of which is a sticky, poisonous substance that they will spit or fling at an enemy. Survival rates against these creatures are fairly high, though unprepared prospectors may find themselves in a sticky situation.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hammerhead_Slug-Lore3", "\"Oh god, this stuff stinks. Stu, how long did it take you to get the goop out of your suit? I don\'t have a spare...\"")

Rows.Name: MiniHippo
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "MiniHippo-Lore1", "The result of another of Dr Lee\'s unsanctioned experiments, the Dribbo as it\'s been named by prospectors has become somewhat of an example of when science goes too far. Illegal genetic splicing of Earth hippo DNA and Icarus native DNA resulted in these curious-looking creatures. Once released planetside they were quick to reproduce, but the influence of Exotics quickly mutated some offspring.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "MiniHippo-Lore2", "All Dribbo share beak-like mouth features, however some Exotic-affected creatures slowly developed an odd spiked protrusion. Research has shown that this is the egg that the creature hatches from, which they then continue to carry with them to maturity. In an early memo to Group 15 Dr Lee describes this Exotic mutation as \"inevitable\".")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "MiniHippo-Lore3", "\"Nah mate, nah, hear me out... \'Dribbo\' - get it? Cause it\'s a drunk hippo. I\'m calling it now, it\'ll catch on, the other Aussies will love it.\"")

Rows.Name: Sheep
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sheep-Lore1", "The humble Sheep is a mostly peaceful herbivore characterized by its thick fleece, providing insulation against the ever-changing weather of Icarus. Though larger and more robust than Earthen sheep, it retains the gentle demeanor and affinity for grazing common to its kind. Contractors may find solace in their presence, as their wool offers warmth and comfort amidst the harsh expanse of its habitat.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sheep-Lore2", "This breed of Sheep was initially sourced from genetic material of animals from New Zealand. Known for its large Sheep population and wide, expansive farmland, New Zealand was considered the planet\'s best site from which to draw from. Most contractors report that they are easy to work with, and show minimal signs of temper. Though depending on the weather, reports do show that they can be a bit stubborn.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sheep-Lore3", "\"Ah c\'mon Bessie there\'s a good- no, no not that way, Bessie please, we\'ve been over this. It\'s just a haircut, it\'s getting chillier and I need your wool- OOF. Hey, get back here!\"")

Rows.Name: Chicken
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chicken-Lore1", "Chickens may seem like an unlikely creature to exist on Icarus, but after their initial introduction, they have been found to thrive under the care of competent prospectors. They also have an uncanny ability to recognize humans, and hold grudges. Breeder beware!")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chicken-Lore2", "Chickens were introduced on Icarus as part of the Prospector Retention Program. Initially they were provided as a source of nourishment however early trials showed that many prospectors refused to use the animals as food, bonding with them in their solitude on the surface.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chicken-Lore3", "\"Henrietta here has made a huge difference in my time planetside. She\'s been a cute clucky little companion and more importantly a good source of eggs. I know she\'ll stop laying eventually but... we\'ll cross that road when we get to it I guess.\"")

Rows.Name: Kiwi
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kiwi-Lore1", "Kiwis are flightless birds native to New Zealand on Earth. They are characterized by their small size, round body, long beak, and distinctive nocturnal habits. With highly developed senses of smell and touch, they forage for various insects and fruits under the cover of darkness. On Earth, Kiwis play a crucial ecological role as seed dispersers, contributing to the health and diversity of their ecosystems. It was hoped that they would do the same on Icarus after the terraforming failed to spread various plants across the face of the planet.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kiwi-Lore2", "Kiwis were introduced to Icarus to help diversify the plant life in their habitats. They were hunted almost to extinction back on Earth, as they are mostly unable to defend themselves from predators. It is thought that they will likely suffer a similar fate on Icarus, though scientists remain hopeful that the influence of Exotics may trigger the formation of a stronger survival instinct in these birds; only time will tell.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Kiwi-Lore3", "\"I was telling Dave the other day, I don\'t care how much national pride you have for \'em, those things are downright useless. No meat on \'em and they can\'t even fly! So what if I\'m from Australia-\"")

Rows.Name: Ape
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape-Lore1", "The Garganutan is the direct result of unsanctioned experimentation conducted on the surface of Icarus. Researchers were trying to gather data on the effect of exotics on the brain, and whether it could increase the capability of some animals to learn faster, or to a greater extent.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape-Lore2", "Garganutans will often use their surroundings to their advantage in a fight. This is due to their chemically enlarged hippocampus. Because of their enhanced intellect, most prospectors will abandon any conflict or interaction with these creatures out of fear for their safety.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape-Lore3", "\"Rounding up a few creatures here and there is one thing but... a lab experiment that broke out? No thank you, I\'ll stick to mining exotics the hard way after this one.\"")

Rows.Name: Ape_Juvenile
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape_Juvenile-Lore1", "The Garganutan is the direct result of unsanctioned experimentation conducted on the surface of Icarus. Researchers were trying to gather data on the effect of exotics on the brain, and whether it could increase the capability of some animals to learn faster, or to a greater extent.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape_Juvenile-Lore2", "Garganutans will often use their surroundings to their advantage in a fight. This is due to their chemically enlarged hippocampus. Because of their enhanced intellect, most prospectors will abandon any conflict or interaction with these creatures out of fear for their safety.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Ape_Juvenile-Lore3", "\"Rounding up a few creatures here and there is one thing but... a lab experiment that broke out? No thank you, I\'ll stick to mining exotics the hard way after this one.\"")

Rows.Name: Cat
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cat-Lore1", "These cats are felines that have been cryogenically preserved, their sleek fur untouched by their time spent in space. Emerging from stasis, they retain their natural grace and keen senses, navigating their new home with a sense of curiosity and playfulness.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cat-Lore2", "Introduced as part of the Contractor Companion Program, these cats were chosen for their bonding abilities and drive to hunt. Unfortunately, after the first round of trials, it was discovered that the cryo-sleep had destroyed the latter, resulting in some exceptionally lazy cats. However, most contractors reported that they didn\'t mind this and were horrified when asked if they\'d prefer a replacement. ")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cat-Lore3", "\"They seem to be adapting well to th-jfghlk ijdpfg83h4nds iopsfldg WHISKERS! GET OFF THERE\"")

Rows.Name: Dog
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Dog-Lore1", "Dogs are loyal and beloved domesticated mammals known for their unwavering devotion and diverse breeds. With a keen sense of smell and boundless energy, these affectionate animals enrich the lives of humans as faithful companions and trusted helpers, embodying the timeless bond between humans and their four-legged friends back on Earth.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Dog-Lore2", "Introduced as part of the Contractor Companion Program, these dogs were chosen for their bonding abilities with humans. Icarus is known to be a harsh and unforgiving environment, where loneliness can set in quickly. These dogs were frozen in cryo-sleep and transported planetside where they were allocated to select contractors for preliminary trials. These were wildly successful, with many contractors refusing to return the animals at the end of the testing phase.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Dog-Lore3", "\"WOOF! WOOOOOOOF WOOF WOOOOOF WOOF WOOF- Bessie?! How on earth did you get in here- ARE THOSE MY SOCKS?!\"")

Rows.Name: Horse_Standard
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse_Standard-Lore1", "Horses are large, herbivorous mammals renowned for their domestication and utility across various civilizations throughout history on Earth. Characterized by a robust body, a flowing mane, and a distinctive gallop, horses are esteemed for their strength, agility, and adaptability. Upon Icarus, they are a welcome and familiar sight in a place of peril.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse_Standard-Lore2", "Horses were introducted to Icarus as part of the Contractor Companion Program, which was an attempt to encourage Prospectors to stay on Icarus for longer stretches and reduce the overall turnover rate by the UDA. It was fairly successful, and many contractors who received a Horse as part of the program chose to keep them at its conclusion. However, those who lost their companions in planetside accidents were more likely to leave Icarus for good, citing severe emotional distress in their exit interviews.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Horse_Standard-Lore3", "\"Reginald here isn\'t just a steed, though he\'s very handy at helping me move around the place. He\'s got such a personality, I\'d have never have bothered with getting myself a horse back home but now... now I see why so many people love them so darn much.\"")

Rows.Name: Arctic_Moa
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Moa-Lore1", "Found in icy terrazone environments. This previously extinct creature had many variants. Specifically found on Icarus is the Dinornis novaezealandiae, the second largest moa species in recorded history.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Moa-Lore2", "This large flightless bird is the result of genetic experimentation, preserved Moa DNA was found in amber near Tekapo, New Zealand. This was used to revive the extinct species.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Arctic_Moa-Lore3", "\"Life will find a way... Directly into a prospectors stomach.\"")

Rows.Name: Cattle
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cattle-Lore1", "Cattle are hardy, domesticated herbivores renowned for their strength and adaptability, that thrive in grassy areas. Valued for their milk, meat, and labor, they are a cornerstone of agricultural life, providing sustenance and support to humans.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cattle-Lore2", "Cattle are social animals and form strong bonds with other members of their herd. They even have best friends and can become stressed when separated from them. They also have excellent memories and can remember faces, locations, and even the people who treat them well or poorly.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Cattle-Lore3", "\"My pops back home used to run a farm before the climate crisis wiped everything out. Looking after the cattle here reminds me of him.\"")

Rows.Name: RockGolem
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem-Lore1", "Believed to be native to Icarus, the Quarrite is a peculiar creature that upon first glance appears to be made of rocks. This is its armor which must first be removed in order to damage the creature. It can often be found in arid, rocky terrain which helps it to blend in with its surroundings.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem-Lore2", "Quarrites have a rather remarkable skill which consists of consuming entire mineral deposits to strengthen and replenish their armor-like coating. They then use this armor to trample or roll over their prey, to great effect.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem-Lore3", "\"Creatures of interest? No one said anything about banging my head against a literal rock!\"")

Rows.Name: RockGolem_Juvenile
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem_Juvenile-Lore1", "Believed to be native to Icarus, the Quarrite is a peculiar creature that upon first glance appears to be made of rocks. This is its armor which must first be removed in order to damage the creature. It can often be found in arid, rocky terrain which helps it to blend in with its surroundings.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem_Juvenile-Lore2", "Quarrites have a rather remarkable skill which consists of consuming entire mineral deposits to strengthen and replenish their armor-like coating. They then use this armor to trample or roll over their prey, to great effect.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "RockGolem_Juvenile-Lore3", "\"Creatures of interest? No one said anything about banging my head against a literal rock!\"")

Rows.Name: IceMammoth
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth-Lore1", "The Rimetusk is an example of what happens when scientific protocol is ignored. An apprentice of Dr Lee (his whereabouts currently unknown) was tasked with researching chemical interactions with local flora and fauna within the Prometheus region. He instead chose to conduct his own enzyme research. This did not end well for him, or his team.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth-Lore2", "Rimetusks are incredibly strong, with thick ice-like armor covering their vulnerable areas. They live in harmony with flying creatures and will often summon them to help in a fight. Their stomp is a powerful attack that should always be avoided if you want to walk away with your life.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth-Lore3", "\"Did you see the SIZE of that thing? Screw it, I\'m out. I am never taking another Norex contract again!\"")

Rows.Name: IceMammoth_WorldSpawn
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth_WorldSpawn-Lore1", "Revived ancient earth species. A well preserved sample was found deep in the Le Brea tar pits, the first few mammoths were carried by elephants as surrogate mothers. Due to significant debate on the ethics of the mammoths revival, the experiment had to be conducted on Icarus. ")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth_WorldSpawn-Lore2", "Extremist group TPS was responsible for the organisation of global protests against the revival of this species. While this study was not the most advanced genetics experiment, it became a pivotal reference in arguments against genetic augmentation. A slippery slope to experimentation on humans.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "IceMammoth_WorldSpawn-Lore3", "\"While we appreciate new technology that allows us to maintain a healthy ecosystem, the potential of this technology being used unethically... more than likely it already is used unethically. We deserve better, you deserve better.\" - TPS Spokesperson")

Rows.Name: Snow_Slug
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Slug-Lore1", "The UDA regrets to inform you that there is minimal research available on this creature. It appears to be the result of prohibited experimentation by an illegal branch of the company Norex- [REDACTED]")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Slug-Lore2", "This subspecies of Viscid has been observed to attack using frost and seems to be quite resistant to fire. The mutation facilitated by [REDACTED] has increased its strength and gene- [The remainder of this report is illegible and covered in slime]")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Snow_Slug-Lore3", "\"A viscid? Out here? Oh damn it, it hit me! Jax wait, I can\'t move!\"")

Rows.Name: Mange_Wolf
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mange_Wolf-Lore1", "Lean and feral, Skulmutts prowl the irradiated wastes in packs, their skin thinned and split until the bone of their skulls gleams through like pale armor. Their movements are twitchy and erratic - fast when they strike, faster when they flee - and their howls have a warped, metallic timbre that echoes across dead valleys. They show signs of pack coordination and uncanny intelligence, often circling prey into open ground before attacking from multiple angles.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mange_Wolf-Lore2", "DNA traces suggest they\'re the remnants of early canine stock, released to help control small wildlife during the first colonization phase. But radiation and exotic exposure rewrote them - nervous systems overclocked, aggression amplified, bone plating erupting where fur once grew. They thrive in areas most life avoids, drinking from runoff that would kill anything else within hours.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Mange_Wolf-Lore3", "\"Them creepy lookin\' things refuse to walk away if they see you\'re injured.\"")

Rows.Name: GhostCrocodile
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "GhostCrocodile-Lore1", "Trapjaws are semi-subterranean reptilian predators endemic to the deep desert, notorious among prospectors as \"Ghost Crocs.\" Their physiology allows them to dive beneath loose sand and \"swim\" through it at alarming speeds, using powerful lateral undulations and hardened dorsal plates to stay submerged. Despite their size and aggression, Trapjaws have surprisingly poor surface awareness-low perception that often results in sudden, chaotic ambushes triggered more by vibration than sight. They\'ll scavenge readily, but once agitated they behave like full predators, erupting from the sand in explosive lunges.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "GhostCrocodile-Lore2", "The species evolved around shifting dune basins where visibility and stability are unreliable. Over time, reliance on conventional senses diminished as they adapted instead to read pressure waves, heat gradients, and ground resonance. Their dense armor and elongated snout reflect a lifestyle built around brief, violent bursts from the substrate-ideally before prey even knows they\'re there.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "GhostCrocodile-Lore3", "\"Damn near thought the sand was breathing. Turns out it was just one of them Ghost Crocs deciding which one of us to eat first.\"")

Rows.Name: Reaver
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Reaver-Lore1", "This creature is often hard to spot in its swampy habitat as it spends most of its time submerged. This gives it the element of surprise which has unfortunately resulted in much missing data from researchers sent out into the field.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Reaver-Lore2", "These creatures have poor vision but a keen sense of hearing, and are almost impossible to kill while submerged. Some prospectors have taken to leaving an animal corpse nearby to lure them into the open in order to run to safety or to finally finish them off.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Reaver-Lore3", "\"Imagine a massive lobster, but it\'s evil. That\'s a Reaver.\"")

Rows.Name: Drone
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Drone-Lore1", "TPS Drones are armed aerial units deployed across contested regions, operating with a mix of autonomous systems and remote piloting. Designed for relentless engagement, they pursue targets until destroyed, showing no capacity for retreat or self-preservation. Their constant movement, precision fire, and ability to reposition quickly make them a persistent threat, especially in open terrain.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Drone-Lore2", "Developed and fielded by the People\'s Syndicate, these drones reflect TPS\'s shift from protest and propaganda to direct action. Built from scavenged industrial components and repurposed military hardware, the drones embody the faction\'s philosophy: expendable machines fighting a war of ideas.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Drone-Lore3", "\"TPS are either all completely crazy, or fighting for our freedom. You be the judge.\"")

Rows.Name: Orka
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Orka-Lore1", "Stocky, low to the ground, and armored with jagged dorsal spines, the Skulk is a predator built for ambush in the frozen wastes. Its dense, muscle-packed frame lets it plow through snowdrifts with surprising speed, while its thick hide shrugs off most projectiles. It hunts by sound and vibration, often lurking beneath snow until prey strays too close - then erupting upward in a flurry of claws and teeth.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Orka-Lore2", "Genetic analysis suggests the Skulk originated from an early terraforming fauna program - possibly a hybrid between an armored boar and a tundra bear, later warped by long-term exposure to radiation-heavy exotics. The result is a cold-adapted apex scavenger, too stubborn to die and too mean to care.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Orka-Lore3", "\"By the time you hear the ice crunching behind you, it\'s already got your leg.\"")

Rows.Name: Bounder_Desert
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bounder_Desert-Lore1", "Small, deer-like herbivore adapted to desert survival. Korrin have unusually large ears and eyes, likely for detecting predators and regulating body temperature in open terrain. A tiny snout hints at selective grazing on sparse desert vegetation. Extremely flighty, Korrin will bolt at the slightest hint of danger.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bounder_Desert-Lore2", "Introduced to desert zones as part of early terraforming efforts, Korrin have thrived in harsh, open environments. Some isolated populations show minor mutations from prolonged exposure to exotics, enhancing their sensory organs for survival in the heat and sandstorms.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Bounder_Desert-Lore3", "\"One twitch of an ear and it\'s gone. These things survive on nerves and luck alone.\"")

Rows.Name: Tundra_Deer
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Deer-Lore1", "A medium-sized tundra herbivore, the Drosik grazes on sparse grasses and lichen while remaining highly alert to threats. Its single long horn curves backward over its head, seemingly more for display than defense. Extremely skittish, Drosik flee at the slightest disturbance, making them difficult to approach.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Deer-Lore2", "Introduced to tundra regions by early scientists as a grazing species, the Drosik has since adapted to the harsh conditions on-planet. Some populations show subtle mutations from prolonged exposure to exotics, slightly increasing horn length and herd vigilance.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Deer-Lore3", "\"One startled, and the whole herd vanished like smoke. Don\'t waste time trying to sneak up-they\'ve been watching long before you got here.\"")

Rows.Name: Hopping_Creature
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature-Lore1", "Low-slung and wide-framed, Hoppers move in a series of explosive bounds that make them hard to track and even harder to avoid. Their wing-like forelimbs don\'t grant true flight, but they act as stabilizers and shock absorbers when landing, letting the creature ricochet across swamp muck or volcanic scree with unsettling agility. Small but vicious, they favor ambushes - launching themselves straight at a target\'s torso or face.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature-Lore2", "Believed to descend from a terraforming pest-control species, Hoppers were originally meant to keep invasive insects in check. Exposure to exotic particulates in geothermal regions combined with radiation spikes in the swamplands accelerated their musculature and destabilized their equilibrium systems. The result is a creature that moves more like a spring-loaded trap than an animal, striking anything that enters its perceived \"pounce radius.\"")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature-Lore3", "\"If you see the mud twitch, it\'s already too late. They don\'t stalk you - they just appear, teeth first.\"")

Rows.Name: SulfurWorm
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SulfurWorm-Lore1", "Sulfur Worms are colossal burrowing invertebrates that lurk beneath the crust of geothermal basins, using sulfur pools as both camouflage and incubator. Most of their bulk stays submerged, but when prey wanders too close, they surge upward in a violent eruption of steam and acid vapors. Their primary attack is projecting sticky, sulfur-rich globs that are best avoided.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SulfurWorm-Lore2", "Originally adapted to subsistence in sulfuric aquifers, these worms underwent drastic changes in geothermal regions where exotics accumulate underground. The combination of extreme heat, toxic chemistry, and radiation accelerated their growth patterns and converted their natural defensive secretions into hyper-adhesive projectiles. The mutation also heightened their territorial instincts - anything approaching their pools is treated as a threat.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SulfurWorm-Lore3", "\"And here I was thinking the Sandworms were the worst of \'em...\"")

Rows.Name: Slinker
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Slinker-Lore1", "The Slinker prowls the tundra biome, its sleek frame and low stance built for silent pursuit across frozen plains. Its deep blue coat and faint stripes make it nearly invisible in twilight, striking fast from cover. Highly aggressive, it hunts alone, preferring ambush tactics over drawn-out chases.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Slinker-Lore2", "Genetic records suggest the Slinker originated from a hybridized predator species introduced during early terraforming trials. Adaptations to extreme cold (including heat-retentive fur and enhanced musculature) made it one of the few apex predators to thrive post-collapse. Attempts to contain the population have failed repeatedly.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Slinker-Lore3", "\"If you see that shadow moving through the snow, it\'s already too late. The damn thing\'s watching you.\"")

Rows.Name: Raptor
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor-Lore1", "The Raptor is a swift, bipedal predator found across the desert and geothermal biomes. Built for speed and endurance, it uses powerful legs and curved talons to bring down prey with precision strikes. Despite its size, it moves with surprising agility, making it one of the deadliest hunters on Icarus.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor-Lore2", "Originally bred as a terraforming control species to manage invasive herbivores, the Raptor adapted rapidly to the planet\'s extremes. Desert variants developed lighter hides and heat-resistant scales, while geothermal strains evolved thicker skin and increased aggression. Prospectors have since discovered ways to tame and ride them-at considerable risk.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor-Lore3", "\"Tamed one once. Fastest ride you\'ll ever have-right up until it remembers it\'s the apex, and you\'re just lunch with reins.\"")

Rows.Name: Irradiated_Prospector
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Prospector-Lore1", "Spectres tend to wander radioactive zones, their body warped and decayed by prolonged exposure to exotic contamination. Once human, these figures now move with jerky, unnatural precision, glowing faintly in the dark with residual energy. Their claws and distorted limbs make close encounters lethal, and radiation levels spike wherever they roam.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Prospector-Lore2", "Believed to be prospectors who lingered too long near unstable exotic deposits, Spectres are grim evidence of Icarus\' mutagenic potential. The exotic matter fused with organic tissue, keeping the host animated long past viability. Whether driven by instinct, agony, or the pull of the exotics themselves remains unknown.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Prospector-Lore3", "\"If that\'s what\'s left of us after the job... maybe the bonus ain\'t worth it.\"")

Rows.Name: Chew
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chew-Lore1", "Dravens are hardy, thick-skinned desert dwellers built to endure the scorching winds and blistering heat of Icarus\'s arid biomes. Their muscular bodies store both water and energy, allowing them to roam for days without rest. Normally docile, they can become aggressive when threatened - and their powerful legs make them dangerous foes up close.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chew-Lore2", "Descended from Earth camels genetically modified for off-world colonization, Dravens were designed to survive extreme dehydration and radiation exposure. Over time, environmental adaptation on Icarus has thickened their hides and altered their physiology, making them far more resilient - and far less patient - than their ancestors.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Chew-Lore3", "\"Don\'t let the big eyes fool you. I tried to pet one once - still can\'t bend my wrist right.\"")

Rows.Name: Hopping_Creature_Swamp
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature_Swamp-Lore1", "Low-slung and wide-framed, Hoppers move in a series of explosive bounds that make them hard to track and even harder to avoid. Their wing-like forelimbs don\'t grant true flight, but they act as stabilizers and shock absorbers when landing, letting the creature ricochet across swamp muck or volcanic scree with unsettling agility. Small but vicious, they favor ambushes - launching themselves straight at a target\'s torso or face.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature_Swamp-Lore2", "Believed to descend from a terraforming pest-control species, Hoppers were originally meant to keep invasive insects in check. Exposure to exotic particulates in geothermal regions combined with radiation spikes in the swamplands accelerated their musculature and destabilized their equilibrium systems. The result is a creature that moves more like a spring-loaded trap than an animal, striking anything that enters its perceived \"pounce radius.\"")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Hopping_Creature_Swamp-Lore3", "\"If you see the mud twitch, it\'s already too late. They don\'t stalk you - they just appear, teeth first.\"")

Rows.Name: Raptor_Desert
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor_Desert-Lore1", "The Raptor is a swift, bipedal predator found across the desert and geothermal biomes. Built for speed and endurance, it uses powerful legs and curved talons to bring down prey with precision strikes. Despite its size, it moves with surprising agility, making it one of the deadliest hunters on Icarus.")
