Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor_Desert-Lore2", "Originally bred as a terraforming control species to manage invasive herbivores, the Raptor adapted rapidly to the planet\'s extremes. Desert variants developed lighter hides and heat-resistant scales, while geothermal strains evolved thicker skin and increased aggression. Prospectors have since discovered ways to tame and ride them-at considerable risk.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Raptor_Desert-Lore3", "\"It ain\'t a sandworm... but it\'ll do.\"")

Rows.Name: Stomper
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Stomper-Lore1", "Standing tall on wiry legs and crowned with bright plumage, the Stomper is an elegant creature. Its oversized talons and reinforced leg bones make it deceptively powerful, capable of breaking stone or skulls with a single strike. Normally, the Stomper wanders volcanic plains in small herds, pecking through ash for mineral-rich roots and insects. When threatened, though, it unleashes thunderous stomps that can crack stone.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Stomper-Lore2", "Believed to have descended from Earth\'s ratites, the Stomper\'s physiology shows clear exotic-radiation mutation - the heat-resistant feathers and shock-dampening tendons aren\'t natural. Logs suggest these birds were originally introduced for pest control, but prolonged exposure to volatile radiation pockets in the volcanic zone made them far meaner - and much harder to cook.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Stomper-Lore3", "\"Peaceful until you make eye contact. Then it\'s you or the bird.\"")

Rows.Name: Storca
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Storca-Lore1", "Storcas are mid-sized predators native to the tundra, built for agility and endurance in low-visibility conditions. Their lightweight frames, long limbs, and heat-sensing cranial organs allow them to navigate blizzards and frozen terrain without relying on sight. Though cautious and often aggressive toward intruders, Storcas show surprisingly social behavior among their own kind and can be playful in groups, especially when not threatened. With careful training, they can even be domesticated as companions.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Storca-Lore2", "Storcas developed in regions where extreme cold and diffuse exotic exposure disrupted typical sensory evolution. Over generations, reliance on vision diminished, replaced by more specialized detection systems tuned to vibration, heat, and atmospheric shifts. Their cooperative tendencies likely evolved as a survival advantage in harsh, resource-scarce environments.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Storca-Lore3", "\"Reminds me of my old greyhound back home, if you\'re able to make friends with one they\'re kinda cute...\"")

Rows.Name: Giant_Roach
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Giant_Roach-Lore1", "Towering, skeletal insects that move like nightmares on stilts. Clickers stalk the swamp shallows in eerie silence, their translucent limbs swaying with each step. Long tendrils hang from their segmented underbellies, trailing through the air - contact with one delivers a paralytic jolt that leaves most creatures helpless. They aren\'t aggressive by nature, but their height makes them oblivious to smaller life below.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Giant_Roach-Lore2", "Genetic markers indicate a hybridization of terrestrial roach and crustacean genomes, designed to thrive in high-humidity waste zones. Exposure to both exotics and radiation spurred unchecked growth and neurotoxin adaptation - their paralysis effect originally intended to subdue carrion for recycling. Left to breed unchecked, they\'ve become slow-moving hazards, drifting through the mire like ambulatory traps.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Giant_Roach-Lore3", "\"You don\'t hear them first - you feel it. Legs like scaffolding all around you, and then something brushes your neck... and you faceplant straight into the marsh.\"")

Rows.Name: DragonFly
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "DragonFly-Lore1", "Bright-winged but mean-tempered, Venomflies hover at head height with a jittery, aggressive energy. Their limbs are built for gripping bark and exposed roots, and their oversized red eyes track movement with unnerving precision. While not large enough to be apex predators, they more than make up for it with painful stings and fast, darting flight patterns.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "DragonFly-Lore2", "Originally engineered as a pollinator species for wetland reclamation, Venomflies were meant to resist swampborne parasites and fungal rot. Post-collapse radiation surges interacting with exotic particulate triggered hyper-territorial behavior and an overproduction of neurotoxic venom. Their nests - usually clustered in hollowed logs or dense root tangles - are treated as sacred ground, and even minor vibrations can provoke a swarm response.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "DragonFly-Lore3", "\"One second the swamp\'s quiet, the next something green and angry is trying to jam its stinger in your visor. Step near a nest and you\'ll learn real fast what \'territorial\' means.\"")

Rows.Name: Flying_Tank
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flying_Tank-Lore1", "Ravagers are massive, volcanic-dwelling arthropods that resemble boulders until they decide otherwise. Curled tight, they pass for part of the terrain - a convenient heat sink basking in lava vents. When disturbed, the creature unfurls with startling speed, revealing powerful wings and a mouth built to crack stone.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flying_Tank-Lore2", "These giants trace back to reinforced mining-support organisms originally developed to clear debris in extreme temperatures. In volcanic biomes saturated with exotic particulates and radiation, their physiology pushed into overdrive: chitin hardened into near-ceramic shielding, musculature expanded, and dormant flight structures became fully functional.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flying_Tank-Lore3", "\"When a \'rock\' the size of a cargo container stands up and takes off, you learn real quick which direction not to run.\"")

Rows.Name: Flightless_Tank
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flightless_Tank-Lore1", "Sandhorns are towering, slow-moving desert leviathans whose shells look more like eroded mesas than living armor. Their massive front horn is used primarily for shoving aside boulders and uprooting scrub, but it becomes a devastating weapon if provoked. Despite their size, Sandhorns are herbivores with calm dispositions - provided you don\'t startle them, corner them, or sneeze too loudly within a five-meter radius.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flightless_Tank-Lore2", "Introduced as part of early terraforming experiments, Sandhorns were meant to stabilize shifting dunes and aerate soil as they grazed. Exotics interacted with their mineral-rich diet, reinforcing their shells into near-geologic hardness. Over time this made them incredibly resilient but also incredibly stubborn, a combination that made Group 15 relocation efforts... brief.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Flightless_Tank-Lore3", "\"If you think a Sandhorn looks slow, wait until you make it mad.\"")

Rows.Name: Sandwyrm_Queen
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandwyrm_Queen-Lore1", "Sandwyrm Queens are colossal, airborne predators that dominate the deep desert and represent the final stage of the sandworm life cycle. Their immense size is substantial enough to interfere with satellite signals, and their armored exoskeleton shrugs off most conventional weapons. As matriarchs, they are responsible for laying clutches of eggs that eventually hatch into the sandworms that burrow unseen beneath the dunes. Highly territorial and violently aggressive, a Queen will respond to any perceived threat with overwhelming force. Despite their near-impenetrable hide, the cranial region and mouth cavity remain the only reliable vulnerabilities.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandwyrm_Queen-Lore2", "Unlike many modern threats on Icarus, Sandwyrm Queens are not exotic mutations but remnants of the planet\'s original biosphere-one of the few apex species resilient enough to survive failed terraforming and the collapse of native ecosystems. Their ability to fly, reproduce rapidly, and endure extreme arid climates gave them a significant evolutionary edge. Queens rarely appear on the surface unless provoked or during reproductive cycles, but when they do, their presence can destabilize entire regions.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Sandwyrm_Queen-Lore3", "\"WHAT DO YOU MEAN THEY CAN FLY NOW?!\" ")

Rows.Name: Geothermal_Bat
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Bat-Lore1", "Sulfur Vespers are bat-like aerial pests commonly found in geothermal regions, nesting around vents, caves, and unstable rock formations. They are highly aggressive and will swarm anything that enters their territory, relying on rapid flight and repeated dive attacks. While individually fragile, their numbers and erratic movement make them a constant hazard in certain zones.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Bat-Lore2", "These creatures are believed to have evolved from small flying mammals that adapted to geothermal environments. Prolonged exposure to sulfur gases altered their respiratory systems and heightened their aggression, allowing them to thrive where few predators can follow. Their rapid breeding cycles ensure infestations form quickly if left unchecked.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Bat-Lore3", "\"You know what? AGENT was right, everyone hates Vespers...\"")

Rows.Name: Yeti
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Yeti-Lore1", "Frostfoots are bulky, frost-coated omnivores roughly the size of an Earth Grizzly, but far more irritable. Their dense white fur insulates a muscular frame built for short, explosive bursts of speed, and the curved horns serve as both intimidation display and battering implement. Their name comes from their wide, heat-retentive paws, which allow them to traverse ice sheets without slipping - unfortunately, they also allow Frostfoots to stomp a prospector into the snow with unnerving stability.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Yeti-Lore2", "Originally introduced by Norex as a hardy cold-biome work animal prototype, Frostfoots adapted too well. Prolonged exposure to exotic-laced permafrost altered their metabolism, increasing aggression and muscle density beyond what handlers could safely manage. After several \"containment failures\" (read: they tore down the fences and ate the researchers), the program was quietly abandoned. The animals thrived without supervision, much to everyone else\'s dismay.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Yeti-Lore3", "\"Yeah I had a friend back on Earth who was super into Bigfoot, imagine if he\'d seen THESE!\"")

Rows.Name: Irradiated_Abomination
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Abomination-Lore1", "Revenants represent the final stage of human-afflicted radiation-exotic toxicity-an escalation far beyond Spectres and Wraiths. Barely recognizable as once-human, these creatures have grown massive under prolonged exposure to radioactive exotic concentrations. Their elongated limbs, hardened growths, and erratic movement patterns make them unpredictable and extremely dangerous. Revenants exhibit heightened aggression and resilience, capable of withstanding significant damage while delivering devastating melee attacks with irradiated claws.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Abomination-Lore2", "Like Spectres and Wraiths, Revenants originate from human bodies saturated with exotic particulate-but only in areas where radiation amplifies exotic reactivity to catastrophic levels. Instead of cellular failure, the combination forces continuous mutation. Most afflicted die long before reaching this stage, but a rare few survive long enough for the process to accelerate unchecked. Revenants are found exclusively in the most unstable radioactive zones, drawn to exotic concentrations they can no longer live without.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Abomination-Lore3", "\"Whatever a Revenant used to be, it\'s long gone. At that point the radiation isn\'t killing them, it\'s feeding them.\"")

Rows.Name: SandScuttler
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SandScuttler-Lore1", "Scuttlers are small, scarab-like arthropods common in volcanic biomes, typically found clustering around carcasses. Although passive at first, they display a wary vigilance-remaining still and observing anyone who comes near. Once a threat moves off, they swarm the corpse in quick, coordinated bursts. If a prospector lingers too long or interferes directly, Scuttlers will abandon their usual caution and attack with surprising ferocity for their size.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SandScuttler-Lore2", "Adapted to extreme heat and toxic soil, Scuttlers evolved as opportunistic scavengers in environments where larger predators rarely linger. Their hardened carapace shields them from volcanic particulates, while their group-feeding behavior ensures they can rapidly strip a carcass before competition arrives. In high-activity zones, they\'re often the first sign that something recently died-sometimes very recently.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "SandScuttler-Lore3", "\"They won\'t touch you unless you get between them and a fresh corpse. Problem is, sometimes you\'re the fresh corpse they\'re waiting for.\"")

Rows.Name: Tundra_Monkey
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Monkey-Lore1", "Gribblers are small, fur-covered omnivores that scuttle across the tundra with unnerving speed. Their forward-leaning gait and elongated forelimbs give them a hunched, almost primate-like profile, though whatever face they once had has collapsed into a puckered, tooth-studded maw. They scavenge more than they hunt, but desperation - or opportunity - can drive them into sudden melee scrambles. Some have developed the charming habit of lobbing whatever they can grab: ice chunks, bones, frozen filth... all fair game.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Monkey-Lore2", "Believed to be descendants of an introduced small mammal species, Gribblers adapted rapidly to the tundra\'s scarcity. Exotic exposure didn\'t make them bigger or braver - just stranger. Their sensory organs migrated beneath dense fur for insulation, and their jaws overdeveloped to deal with nutrient-poor diets. Radiation pockets in the region may account for their erratic behavior patterns and occasional, rudimentary ranged attacks.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Tundra_Monkey-Lore3", "\"If it\'s shaking, chittering, and throwing something at you? Congratulations - you\'ve met a Gribbler.\"")

Rows.Name: FleshSac_Floater
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "FleshSac_Floater-Lore1", "Gaseous Floaters are buoyant, sack-like organisms that drift slowly through geothermal regions, suspended by internal gas pockets. Their swollen bodies are laced with volatile compounds, and any sudden impact-particularly contact with the ground-causes an immediate explosive rupture. While they move passively, their unpredictable descent paths make them an ever-present environmental hazard.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "FleshSac_Floater-Lore2", "These organisms are believed to have evolved around geothermal vents where toxic gases are abundant. Over time, their internal structures adapted to store and metabolize volatile compounds for buoyancy, with explosive decomposition becoming an unintended side effect. Exotic exposure appears minimal; their danger comes not from mutation, but from extreme environmental specialization.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "FleshSac_Floater-Lore3", "\"Nothin\' but a sack of farts if you ask me. Go on, poke one and see what happens.\"")

Rows.Name: Irradiated_Mutation
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Mutation-Lore1", "Wraiths are towering, heavily mutated remnants of human origin, their limbs stretched beyond natural limits and bodies studded with crystalline growths. Found deep within irradiated zones, they move with terrifying speed and precision, attacking anything that enters their territory. Contractors report that radiation levels spike sharply when one is near.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Mutation-Lore2", "Unlike ordinary Spectres, Wraiths are the product of extreme exotic contamination amplified by radiation exposure. The unstable fusion of exotic material and decaying human tissue accelerates mutation, reshaping the host into a crystalline predator. Without radiation, the exotics remain inert - but together, they twist biology into something wholly alien.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Irradiated_Mutation-Lore3", "\"If a Spectre\'s what you become, then a Wraith\'s what\'s left when the radiation finishes the job.\"")

Rows.Name: Geothermal_Spider
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Spider-Lore1", "Sulfuric Spiders are large arachnids native to geothermal regions, commonly found near sulfur vents and unstable ground. Their thick, heat-resistant exoskeleton protects them from corrosive gases, while elongated legs allow them to navigate fissures and uneven terrain with ease. Highly aggressive, they will defend their territory on sight, using venomous bites and rapid strikes to overwhelm intruders.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Spider-Lore2", "These spiders evolved in environments saturated with sulfur and extreme heat, where only highly specialized life could persist. Long-term exposure to geothermal gases altered the composition of their venom, making it highly corrosive and fast-acting. Unlike many Icarus creatures, Sulfuric Spiders show little evidence of exotic-driven mutation, suggesting adaptation rather than interference is responsible for their resilience.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Geothermal_Spider-Lore3", "\"Cool, get a spider and make it bigger, angrier and faster. How lovely, I\'m sure everyone will love these... Perhaps next time we could try something a little nicer, like butterflies?\"")

Rows.Name: Pig
Rows.Lore1: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pig-Lore1", "Domesticated swine maintained within controlled habitats to provide a reliable, high-yield protein source for long-term planetary stays. They are surprisingly hardy against minor atmospheric fluctuations but require consistent oversight to prevent them from damaging habitat infrastructure.")
Rows.Lore2: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pig-Lore2", "Transferred as part of the UDA\'s \"Bio-Sustainability Initiative\" via cryogenic embryo units and gestated in onsite labs. These specimens are genetically filtered to favor rapid growth and docility, distinguishing them from more aggressive feral populations found in the wild.")
Rows.Lore3: NSLOCTEXT("D_BestiaryData", "Pig-Lore3", "\"They\'re the most Earth-like thing on this rock, right down to the smell and the stubborn refusal to move when you need them to.\"")
